body:
## Summary

- Add `agent-self-approve` as an internal orchestrator-dispatched PR approval gate gated by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE`.
- Add deterministic CLIs to preflight PR head state, parse structured self-approval verdicts, and submit approval only for matching open PR heads.
- Extend orchestrator handoff policy, delegated authorization checks, prompts, workflow docs, and config docs.
- Add focused tests for self-approval parsing, stale-head rejection, opt-in dispatch, and delegated route authorization.

## Verification

- `npm run build`
- `node --test dist/__tests__/self-approval.test.js dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js dist/__tests__/triage.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrator-preflight-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`
- YAML parse check for `agent-self-approve.yml` and `agent-orchestrator.yml`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #165

comments.body: @sepo-agent /orchestrate there's a merge conflict to main after merging #162 can you check and fix

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| orchestrate | review | PR #166 | 2 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1778171839497 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjU1MDkwNjI0MTM6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MTY2OnJldmlldzoxNjY6Mg -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 90 | PARTIAL | 11 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Initial `/orchestrate` capability checks include `agent-self-approve` only when enabled and validate the requester against route policy: [orchestrator-capabilities.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/orchestrator-capabilities.ts#L55-L67). |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The new follow-up is opt-in and only follows `review -> SHIP`; disabled still stops on `SHIP`: [handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L454-L465). |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 5/6 | Self-approval status uses a short table plus reason/context outside the table: [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L181-L200). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR routes through existing typed CLIs/helpers, `post-response`, `fetchPrMeta`, access policy checks, and orchestrator handoff machinery rather than embedding the behavior in workflow shell. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to the new self-approval route, orchestrator handoff/capability plumbing, docs, prompt, workflow, and focused tests. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Config and workflow docs document `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE` and the new route: [configuration-list.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md#L11-L14). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Workflow inputs remain separate typed fields, and the prompt requires a structured JSON verdict with `verdict`, `reason`, `handoff_context`, and `inspected_head_sha`. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is a small state resolver plus two CLIs; approval is submitted only after deterministic `resolveSelfApproval` checks. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | partial | 3/7 | The body includes `<!-- sepo-agent-self-approval -->`, but the workflow posts via generic `post-response` without looking up/updating that marker: [workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L162-L172), [post-response.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/cli/post-response.ts#L66-L69). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 4/7 | `resolve-self-approve` swallows PR metadata read failures and converts them to empty state/head, later reported as “pull request is not open” or “head changed” instead of surfacing metadata read failure: [resolve-self-approve.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/cli/resolve-self-approve.ts#L65-L85), [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L103-L131). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Disabled or unsupported self-approval runs post a visible stop comment instead of silently doing nothing: [agent-self-approve.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L68-L86). |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric fit is around conservative opt-in behavior: the route is not user-invokable through slash commands, is default-disabled, validates requester authorization when orchestrator delegation could reach it, and rechecks PR head state before approval.
- The main rubric gaps are operational polish: repeated blocked/status runs can create duplicate visible comments, and a late PR metadata read failure is safe but misleadingly reported.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The self-approval status marker is not used for idempotent updates. Rerunning a blocked or non-approved self-approval can create another PR comment even though the body has a stable marker.
- **WARNING:** A metadata read failure during result resolution is swallowed and can be surfaced as “not open” or “head changed,” which weakens retry/debug visibility.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #166 adds an opt-in `agent-self-approve` gate for Sepo, closing #165 and implementing the discussion request for a conservative final PR approval step.
- The intended model is internal/orchestrator-led: after review synthesis returns `SHIP`, a self-approval agent inspects the PR and deterministic code submits approval only when opt-in, PR state, verdict, and head SHA checks pass.
- The PR adds a new workflow, prompt, typed prepare/resolve CLIs, orchestration handoff policy, delegated authorization plumbing, docs, and focused tests.
- The current PR is still unmergeable against `main` after #162, and reviewers found several runtime and safety gaps in the approval path.

## Review
Codex marked the PR `NEEDS_REWORK`, Claude’s overall verdict was `MINOR_ISSUES`, and the rubrics review was `PARTIAL`. This synthesis prioritizes the blockers because GitHub currently reports the PR as `CONFLICTING`/`DIRTY`, and the approval workflow has deterministic runtime and trust-boundary issues.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Merge conflict with main after #162 | BLOCKING | PR #166 is not currently mergeable. The docs conflict needs a rebase that preserves both #162’s comment-collapse wording and this PR’s self-approval documentation. |
| `agent-self-approve` is not a valid runtime route | BLOCKING | The workflow passes `route: agent-self-approve`, but `.agent/src/envelope.ts` does not include that route in `VALID_ROUTES`, so `run.ts` will reject the envelope before the prompt runs. |
| Agent inspection step can mutate GitHub before deterministic checks | BLOCKING | The self-approval agent runs with `permission_mode: approve-all` and a write-capable GitHub token, bypassing the intended guarantee that only deterministic post-processing can approve. |
| Missing `inspected_head_sha` still allows approval | BLOCKING | A blank inspected SHA skips the mismatch check, so an `APPROVE` verdict can still set `shouldApprove: true` without proving which head the agent inspected. |
| Self-approval can bypass the review-to-SHIP handoff | WARNING | Direct `workflow_dispatch` plus current preflight allows enabled self-approval on an open PR without verifying trusted `review -> SHIP` provenance or equivalent review/rubrics signals. |
| Failure handling can hide or misreport approval-state errors | WARNING | Metadata read failures are swallowed, approval API failures can skip PR-visible status, and artifacts are skipped on result-step failure. |
| Self-approval status comments are not idempotent | WARNING | The body has a stable marker, but the workflow posts via generic `post-response` without updating/reusing an existing self-approval comment. |

## Progress
- The PR already implements the conservative default-disabled opt-in flag, PR-only preflight, orchestrator handoff wiring, docs, and focused tests for the main happy and stale-head paths.
- The rubrics review already acknowledged strong alignment on delegated authorization, one-shot default behavior, code reuse, and docs.
- The rubrics review already surfaced the idempotency and metadata-read warnings, so no duplicate inline comments were added for those.
- Inline comments were posted for the unregistered route, write-capable inspection step, missing inspected head SHA, skipped failure status, and direct dispatch handoff bypass.
- Claude’s INFO notes about prompt substitution and approval-review-body asymmetry look intentional and are not included as required action items.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Merge conflict with main after #162</summary>

**Cause:** GitHub reports PR #166 as `mergeable: CONFLICTING` / `mergeStateStatus: DIRTY` after #162 landed adjacent documentation changes.

**Candidate solutions:** Rebase the PR branch onto `main` and reconcile the docs so both `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS` wording and the new `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE` content remain.

**Comments:** Codex treated this as blocking; Claude called it mechanical but still identified it as unresolved.

</details>

<details>
<summary>`agent-self-approve` is not a valid runtime route</summary>

**Cause:** [agent-self-approve.yml#L125](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L125) sends `route: agent-self-approve`, while [envelope.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/envelope.ts#L55) lacks that route in `VALID_ROUTES`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `agent-self-approve` to `VALID_ROUTES` and extend `validateEnvelope` tests to cover it.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Agent inspection step can mutate GitHub before deterministic checks</summary>

**Cause:** [agent-self-approve.yml#L120-L123](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L120) gives the inspection agent a write-capable token and `approve-all` permissions.

**Candidate solutions:** Run the agent inspection step with read-only permissions and reserve the write-capable token for `resolve-self-approve.js`, or otherwise prevent tool-level mutation before deterministic verdict/head checks.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing `inspected_head_sha` still allows approval</summary>

**Cause:** [self-approval.ts#L136](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L136) only blocks when `inspectedHeadSha` is non-empty and mismatched.

**Candidate solutions:** Reject blank `inspected_head_sha` for approval verdicts and add a regression test for blank/missing inspected SHA.

**Comments:** Claude considered this lower risk because current-head still matches expected-head; Codex treated it as blocking because it weakens the stated inspected-head contract.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval can bypass the review-to-SHIP handoff</summary>

**Cause:** [agent-self-approve.yml#L4](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L4) is directly dispatchable, and preflight does not validate trusted orchestration provenance or latest trusted review/rubrics state.

**Candidate solutions:** Require trusted orchestrator context, or deterministically verify a latest trusted `SHIP` review/rubrics signal before approval can proceed.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Failure handling can hide or misreport approval-state errors</summary>

**Cause:** [resolve-self-approve.ts#L68-L75](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/cli/resolve-self-approve.ts#L68) converts PR metadata read failures into empty state/head, and [agent-self-approve.yml#L162-L175](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L162) lacks `always()` handling for result-step failures.

**Candidate solutions:** Surface metadata read failures explicitly, catch approval submission failures into a failed status body, and make status/artifact steps run on resolver failure with focused tests.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval status comments are not idempotent</summary>

**Cause:** [self-approval.ts#L200](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L200) emits a stable marker, but [agent-self-approve.yml#L162-L172](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/80fc3c4a6f010e5be40672cc00b522ae50071ca7/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L162) posts through generic `post-response` without marker lookup/update.

**Candidate solutions:** Reuse or update the existing `<!-- sepo-agent-self-approval -->` comment, or add collapse/update behavior equivalent to existing agent status comment handling.

**Comments:** Already covered clearly in the posted rubrics review.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The unresolved findings require concrete branch changes, and the merge conflict plus deterministic route/safety fixes are suitable for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`NEEDS_REWORK`

## Action Items
- [ ] Rebase/resolve the #162 merge conflict while preserving both #162 and #166 documentation changes.
- [ ] Add `agent-self-approve` to `VALID_ROUTES` and cover it in `validateEnvelope` tests.
- [ ] Run self-approval inspection without write-capable tools/token; reserve approval authority for deterministic resolver code.
- [ ] Reject approval verdicts with missing or blank `inspected_head_sha` and add regression coverage.
- [ ] Enforce trusted `review -> SHIP` provenance or latest trusted review/rubrics signal before approval.
- [ ] Surface metadata and approval submission failures visibly, including status/artifact behavior on resolver failure.
- [ ] Reuse/update the stable self-approval status comment instead of creating duplicate comments.

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| review | fix-pr | PR #166 | 3 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for PR #166; the PR is still CONFLICTING/DIRTY and has concrete unresolved deterministic route/safety findings suitable for an automated fix-pr pass.

Task for fix-pr:
Address only the latest review synthesis action items:
- Rebase/resolve the #162 merge conflict while preserving both #162 and #166 documentation changes.
- Add `agent-self-approve` to `VALID_ROUTES` and cover it in `validateEnvelope` tests.
- Run self-approval inspection without write-capable tools/token; reserve approval authority for deterministic resolver code.
- Reject approval verdicts with missing or blank `inspected_head_sha` and add regression coverage.
- Enforce trusted `review -> SHIP` provenance or latest trusted review/rubrics signal before approval.

Constraints: Ignore optional INFO notes, metadata-only polish, already-fixed findings, and human-judgment nits unless required by those action items.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1778172682810 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjU1MDkxMDE2NjA6cmV2aWV3OjE2NjpmaXhfcHI6MTY2OjM -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-165/codex-25508067922`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

<!-- sepo-agent-fix-pr-status -->

- Merged `origin/main` into the PR branch worktree and resolved the #162 docs conflict while preserving both self-approval docs and `fix-pr` cleanup docs.
- Added `agent-self-approve` to `VALID_ROUTES` with `validateEnvelope` coverage.
- Hardened self-approval: read-only inspection token/tools, required `inspected_head_sha` for approvals, and trusted latest review/rubrics provenance checks in prepare + resolve.
- Left the worktree in a resolved merge state for the workflow commit step; no unmerged paths or conflict markers remain.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm run build`
- `node --test dist/__tests__/self-approval.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrator-preflight-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/post-comment-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/response.test.js dist/__tests__/review-summary-minimize.test.js`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- temporary merge validation confirmed `origin/main` is an ancestor of the resolved merge result

</details>

Sepo is dispatching follow-up automation.

| Source | Next | Target | Round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| fix-pr | review | PR #166 | 4 / 40 | Dispatched |

Reason: agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded on PR #166, merge state is now CLEAN, checks are passing, and the prior review had concrete blocking findings that should be revalidated after the branch changes.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1778173677323 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjU1MDk4MDE2OTg6Zml4X3ByOjE2NjpyZXZpZXc6MTY2OjQ -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 91 | PARTIAL | 11 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Initial `/orchestrate` checks include `agent-self-approve` only when enabled and require requester access to that downstream route: [orchestrator-capabilities.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/orchestrator-capabilities.ts#L55-L67). |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | Direct `review -> SHIP` still stops unless `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE` is enabled; self-approval only hands back to `fix-pr` on `REQUEST_CHANGES`: [handoff.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L454-L494). |
| communication / generic | Keep status comments compact | pass | 5/6 | Self-approval status uses a short two-column table plus reason/context text, not raw logs: [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L303-L329). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR uses typed CLIs, existing GitHub helpers, `run-agent-task`, `post-response`, handoff policy, and access-policy plumbing instead of putting nontrivial logic in workflow shell. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to the self-approval route, prompt, workflow, orchestration plumbing, docs, and focused tests; PR is now `MERGEABLE`/`CLEAN` after the conflict fix. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Config and architecture docs now document the opt-in flag, route behavior, read-only inspection token, deterministic approval resolver, and stop conditions: [configuration-list.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md#L11-L14), [agent-orchestrator.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md#L155-L167). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The workflow keeps explicit inputs, and the parser expects structured verdict fields including `verdict`, `reason`, `handoff_context`, and `inspected_head_sha`: [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L160-L187). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is a small resolver/preflight path with direct state checks for opt-in, PR-only target, open state, head SHA, provenance, and verdict: [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L190-L301). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | partial | 4/7 | Status bodies include a stable marker, but the workflow still posts through generic `post-response` without marker lookup/update for self-approval comments: [self-approval.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/self-approval.ts#L328), [agent-self-approve.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L174-L184), [post-response.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/cli/post-response.ts#L46-L68). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | partial | 5/7 | Preflight surfaces metadata/provenance read failures, but result resolution still swallows PR metadata read failures into empty state/head, which later reports a generic closed/stale-head condition; approval submission failure also occurs before outputs/body file are written: [prepare-self-approve.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/cli/prepare-self-approve.ts#L53-L83), [resolve-self-approve.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/cli/resolve-self-approve.ts#L75-L83), [resolve-self-approve.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/cli/resolve-self-approve.ts#L117-L123). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Comment on accepted workflow stops | pass | 6/6 | Disabled, unsupported, stale, missing-provenance, and blocked paths produce visible stop/status bodies instead of silently doing nothing: [prepare-self-approve.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.agent/src/cli/prepare-self-approve.ts#L32-L83), [agent-self-approve.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6/.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml#L70-L88). |

## Notes

- The fix-pr pass addressed the prior blocking rubric gaps: the route is registered, inspection is read-only, approval requires `inspected_head_sha`, and approval provenance is checked before and after inspection.
- Remaining rubric gaps are non-blocking operational polish: self-approval status comments are not idempotently updated, and late resolver failures are still partly generic.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Self-approval status comments can duplicate on reruns because the stable marker is emitted but not used for update/reuse.
- **INFO:** Resolver-side metadata or approval API failures could be surfaced more explicitly with focused tests, but the current behavior blocks approval rather than approving unsafely.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #166 adds an opt-in `agent-self-approve` workflow gated by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE`.
- The broader goal from #165 / discussion #153 is a conservative final PR approval gate, not a general “merge whatever the agent made” mode.
- The design adds structured self-approval verdict parsing, head-SHA checks, orchestrator handoff support, docs, and tests.
- A follow-up fix-pr pass already resolved the merge conflict and several earlier self-approval safety issues, but current reviewers still found trust-boundary and runtime gaps.

## Review
The PR is closer after the fix-pr pass, and GitHub now reports the branch as `MERGEABLE` / `CLEAN` at `f08c84bd0bd891ad14faa98a0c2a3e4a40cb75e6`. It still needs rework before shipping because the self-approval gate can either block forever in the normal App setup or trust stale review provenance.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Self-approval preflight trusts the wrong actor token | BLOCKING | `prepare-self-approve` uses `github.token` to resolve the trusted actor, while trusted review/rubrics comments are posted by the resolved App/PAT token. In the canonical App setup, provenance matching compares `github-actions[bot]` to `sepo-agent-app` and blocks every run. |
| Provenance is not tied to the reviewed head | BLOCKING | The approval gate trusts the latest matching SHIP/PASS issue comment without proving it applies to the current PR head. A stale SHIP comment can authorize approval of later commits. |
| Failure paths can skip visible status and artifacts | WARNING | `submitApproval` can throw before outputs/body are written, and workflow status/artifact steps lack `always()`. Resolver failures can be visible only in Actions logs and lose debugging artifacts. |
| Self-approval status comments are not idempotent | WARNING | The body has `<!-- sepo-agent-self-approval -->`, but posting still goes through generic `post-response` without updating or collapsing the prior self-approval status comment. |

## Progress
- The #162 merge conflict is resolved; the PR is currently `MERGEABLE` / `CLEAN`.
- Earlier blockers for route registration, read-only inspection permissions, missing `inspected_head_sha`, and direct dispatch provenance were addressed in the fix-pr pass.
- Claude’s merge-conflict finding was from an older head and is now fixed; its failure-handling warnings still overlap current code.
- New inline comments were posted for the actor-token blocker, stale-head provenance blocker, and artifact upload failure path.
- Idempotency and status-failure concerns were already clearly covered in existing PR discussion, so duplicate inline comments were avoided.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Self-approval preflight trusts the wrong actor token</summary>

**Cause:** `.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml` runs `prepare-self-approve.js` with `GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}`, but review synthesis and rubrics comments are posted with `steps.auth.outputs.token` from the App/PAT auth path.

**Candidate solutions:** Resolve GitHub auth before prepare and use that token for provenance reads. Keep the agent inspection step on the read-only `github.token` boundary.

**Comments:** Codex treats this as blocking because the normal Sepo App deployment would never pass provenance.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Provenance is not tied to the reviewed head</summary>

**Cause:** `evaluateSelfApprovalProvenance()` looks only at trusted issue comments and verdict text. Issue comments are not commit-scoped, and the function does not compare a reviewed head SHA to `expectedHeadSha`.

**Candidate solutions:** Persist or pass the reviewed head SHA from review synthesis/handoff and require it to match the current expected head. Do not allow rubrics `PASS` alone to replace a current `SHIP` review synthesis.

**Comments:** Rubrics review considered provenance generally improved, but Codex identified the remaining stale-head gap.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Failure paths can skip visible status and artifacts</summary>

**Cause:** `submitApproval()` throws on API failure before outputs/body are written. The post-status and artifact upload steps also lack `always()`, so they are skipped after resolver failure.

**Candidate solutions:** Catch approval submission failures in `resolve-self-approve` and write a failed status body, or make the workflow run status/artifact steps with `always()` and safe fallback body paths.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval status comments are not idempotent</summary>

**Cause:** `formatSelfApprovalBody()` emits a stable marker, but `.github/workflows/agent-self-approve.yml` posts through generic `post-response.js`, which does not update/reuse that marker.

**Candidate solutions:** Add marker-based update/reuse for `<!-- sepo-agent-self-approval -->`, or collapse/update self-approval status comments like the existing review/fix-pr status paths.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The remaining blockers and warnings require concrete branch changes and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`NEEDS_REWORK`

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #166 adds an opt-in `agent-self-approve` workflow gated by `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE`.
- The broader goal from #165 / discussion #153 is a conservative final PR approval gate after review, not a general autonomous merge mode.
- The current head has already resolved earlier blockers around route registration, read-only inspection, required inspected head SHA, trusted review provenance, visible failures, and idempotent status comments.
- The remaining concern is whether the trusted review-head marker actually represents the commit reviewers inspected.

## Review
The PR is close, and rubrics review is now `PASS`, but one trust-boundary issue still needs a branch fix before this can ship. I posted inline comments for the two current actionable findings.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Review provenance can be stamped onto an unreviewed head | BLOCKING | `post-comment.ts` fetches the PR head when synthesis is posted, not when reviewers inspected the diff. A PR head change during review/synthesis can let a stale `SHIP` synthesis authorize self-approval of new commits. |
| Self-approval status upsert can target untrusted marker comments | WARNING | `upsertPrCommentByMarker()` updates the latest comment containing the marker without checking the author. A user-seeded marker can cause Sepo to PATCH the wrong comment or fail to post its own status. |

## Progress
- Current PR head: `e34fc892f2fbab6f05e2e8f56cf28968f5d35249`; GitHub reports `MERGEABLE` / `CLEAN`, with the visible test check passing.
- Earlier review blockers for the merge conflict, route registration, write-capable inspection token, missing `inspected_head_sha`, stale synthesis comments, resolver failure visibility, and duplicate self-approval comments were addressed.
- Claude’s round-6 handoff findings appear stale for the current head: `orchestration_enabled: "true"` is already in `commonInputs`, and `SOURCE_HANDOFF_CONTEXT` is read and forwarded.
- Rubrics review reports `PASS` with no rubric-specific follow-up.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Review provenance can be stamped onto an unreviewed head</summary>

**Cause:** [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/e34fc892f2fbab6f05e2e8f56cf28968f5d35249/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts#L52) calls `fetchPrMeta()` while posting the synthesis comment, after reviewer lanes have already run.

**Candidate solutions:** Capture the PR head SHA before reviewer lanes start, pass that captured SHA through the review/synthesis path, and only emit the trusted synthesis-head marker when it still matches the reviewed head.

**Comments:** This is the remaining blocker because self-approval now trusts the synthesis-head marker as approval provenance.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval status upsert can target untrusted marker comments</summary>

**Cause:** [`upsertPrCommentByMarker()`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/e34fc892f2fbab6f05e2e8f56cf28968f5d35249/.agent/src/github.ts#L240) filters only by marker text, not by authenticated Sepo actor.

**Candidate solutions:** Resolve the authenticated actor and update only matching comments authored by that actor; otherwise create a fresh Sepo-authored status comment.

**Comments:** Existing tests cover marker update behavior, but not the untrusted-marker case.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: One blocking trust-boundary bug and one concrete idempotency hardening issue remain, both suitable for a narrow automated fix.

`NEEDS_REWORK`

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #166 adds an opt-in `agent-self-approve` gate for Sepo, closing #165 and implementing the discussion request for a conservative final PR approval step.
- The intended model is internal/orchestrator-led: after review synthesis returns `SHIP`, a self-approval agent inspects the PR and deterministic code submits approval only when opt-in, PR state, verdict, and head SHA checks pass.
- The PR adds a new workflow, prompt, typed prepare/resolve CLIs, orchestration handoff policy, delegated authorization plumbing, docs, and focused tests.
- The current PR is still unmergeable against `main` after #162, and reviewers found several runtime and safety gaps in the approval path.

## Review
Codex marked the PR `NEEDS_REWORK`, Claude’s overall verdict was `MINOR_ISSUES`, and the rubrics review was `PARTIAL`. This synthesis prioritizes the blockers because GitHub currently reports the PR as `CONFLICTING`/`DIRTY`, and the approval workflow has deterministic runtime and trust-boundary issues.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Merge conflict with main after #162 | BLOCKING | PR #166 is not currently mergeable. The docs conflict needs a rebase that preserves both #162’s comment-collapse wording and this PR’s self-approval documentation. |
| `agent-self-approve` is not a valid runtime route | BLOCKING | The workflow passes `route: agent-self-approve`, but `.agent/src/envelope.ts` does not include that route in `VALID_ROUTES`, so `run.ts` will reject the envelope before the prompt runs. |
| Agent inspection step can mutate GitHub before deterministic checks | BLOCKING | The self-approval agent runs with `permission_mode: approve-all` and a write-capable GitHub token, bypassing the intended guarantee that only deterministic post-processing can approve. |
| Missing `inspected_head_sha` still allows approval | BLOCKING | A blank inspected SHA skips the mismatch check, so an `APPROVE` verdict can still set `shouldApprove: true` without proving which head the agent inspected. |
| Self-approval can bypass the review-to-SHIP handoff | WARNING | Direct `workflow_dispatch` plus current preflight allows enabled self-approval on an open PR without verifying trusted `review -> SHIP` provenance or equivalent review/rubrics signals. |
| Failure handling can hide or misreport approval-state errors | WARNING | Metadata read failures are swallowed, approval API failures can skip PR-visible status, and artifacts are skipped on result-step failure. |
| Self-approval status comments are not idempotent | WARNING | The body has a stable marker, but the workflow posts via generic `post-response` without updating/reusing an existing self-approval comment. |

## Progress
- The PR already implements the conservative default-disabled opt-in flag, PR-only preflight, orchestrator handoff wiring, docs, and focused tests for the main happy and stale-head paths.
- The rubrics review already acknowledged strong alignment on delegated authorization, one-shot default behavior, code reuse, and docs.
- The rubrics review already surfaced the idempotency and metadata-read warnings, so no duplicate inline comments were added for those.
- Inline comments were posted for the unregistered route, write-capable inspection step, missing inspected head SHA, skipped failure status, and direct dispatch handoff bypass.
- Claude’s INFO notes about prompt substitution and approval-review-body asymmetry look intentional and are not included as required action items.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Merge conflict with main after #162</summary>

**Cause:** GitHub reports PR #166 as `mergeable: CONFLICTING` / `mergeStateStatus: DIRTY` after #162 landed adjacent documentation changes.

**Candidate solutions:** Rebase the PR branch onto `main` and reconcile the docs so both `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS` wording and the new `AGENT_ALLOW_SELF_APPROVE` content remain.

**Comments:** Codex treated this as blocking; Claude called it mechanical but still identified it as unresolved.

</details>

<details>
<summary>`agent-self-approve` is not a valid runtime route</summary>

**Cause:** `agent-self-approve.yml` sends `route: agent-self-approve`, while `envelope.ts` lacks that route in `VALID_ROUTES`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `agent-self-approve` to `VALID_ROUTES` and extend `validateEnvelope` tests to cover it.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Agent inspection step can mutate GitHub before deterministic checks</summary>

**Cause:** `agent-self-approve.yml` gives the inspection agent a write-capable token and `approve-all` permissions.

**Candidate solutions:** Run the agent inspection step with read-only permissions and reserve the write-capable token for `resolve-self-approve.js`, or otherwise prevent tool-level mutation before deterministic verdict/head checks.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing `inspected_head_sha` still allows approval</summary>

**Cause:** `self-approval.ts` only blocks when `inspectedHeadSha` is non-empty and mismatched.

**Candidate solutions:** Reject blank `inspected_head_sha` for approval verdicts and add a regression test for blank/missing inspected SHA.

**Comments:** Claude considered this lower risk because current-head still matches expected-head; Codex treated it as blocking because it weakens the stated inspected-head contract.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval can bypass the review-to-SHIP handoff</summary>

**Cause:** `agent-self-approve.yml` is directly dispatchable, and preflight does not validate trusted orchestration provenance or latest trusted review/rubrics state.

**Candidate solutions:** Require trusted orchestrator context, or deterministically verify a latest trusted `SHIP` review/rubrics signal before approval can proceed.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Failure handling can hide or misreport approval-state errors</summary>

**Cause:** `resolve-self-approve.ts` converts PR metadata read failures into empty state/head, and `agent-self-approve.yml` lacks `always()` handling for result-step failures.

**Candidate solutions:** Surface metadata read failures explicitly, catch approval submission failures into a failed status body, and make status/artifact steps run on resolver failure with focused tests.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Self-approval status comments are not idempotent</summary>

**Cause:** `self-approval.ts` emits a stable marker, but `agent-self-approve.yml` posts through generic `post-response` without marker lookup/update.

**Candidate solutions:** Reuse or update the existing `<!-- sepo-agent-self-approval -->` comment, or add collapse/update behavior equivalent to existing agent status comment handling.

**Comments:** Already covered clearly in the posted rubrics review.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The unresolved findings require concrete branch changes, and the merge conflict plus deterministic route/safety fixes are suitable for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`NEEDS_REWORK`
